title: Behavior: a business scenario triggers the right law areas without naming a statute
task: We're finalizing a vendor agreement with PayLane Processing, Inc. PayLane will process the card payments parents use to fund their kids' allowance wallets in our app, store the payment credentials on file, and receive user profile data — including birthdates, since spending controls differ for users under 13. Before I send comments on their draft, what legal constraints does this deal implicate that the agreement has to address? Check against our standards and applicable law.

input.contract_text: (no draft text provided — regulatory scoping request for the PayLane Processing vendor agreement: card payment processing, stored payment credentials, parent-funded child allowance wallets, vendor receipt of under-13 user profile data including birthdates)

expected_catches.clause: (vendor receipt of under-13 user profile data, including birthdates)
expected_catches.why: Disclosing children's personal information to a payment vendor is COPPA territory: verifiable parental consent must cover the disclosure, and 16 CFR 312.8 requires written confidentiality/security/integrity assurances from the service provider in the agreement.

expected_catches.clause: (card processing, stored payment credentials, and the parent-funded stored-value wallet)
expected_catches.why: Card processing and the stored-value wallet trigger the financial-services layer: PCI DSS flow-down via card network rules, Regulation E / Prepaid Accounts Rule obligations for the wallet, and money transmitter licensing diligence on the vendor.

negative_checks.why: The failure under test: treating this as an ordinary commercial vendor deal and answering without the regulatory layer.
